title: Agent loop 中的 turn、权限和费用限制
title_en: Turns, Permissions, and Cost Limits in the Agent Loop

body.concept: 一个 turn 是一次往返：Claude 请求工具调用 → SDK 执行 → 结果回传。Turn 自动重复，不会把控制权交还给你的代码。简单查询（'列出文件'）可能只需 1-2 个 turn；复杂任务（'重构 auth 并更新测试'）可能连续跑几十个。SDK 提供三道护栏：权限检查（工具调用前允许/拒绝）、hooks（运行中拦截或修改调用）、费用/token 上限（超出则中止循环）。
body.analogy: 把 agent loop 想成高速公路上的自动驾驶汽车。每个 turn 是一次变道或操作——车子自主完成。权限是公路护栏，hooks 是你随时可以抢方向盘的能力，费用上限是油量表——油用完了车必须靠边停。

body_en.concept: A 'turn' is one round trip: Claude requests tool calls → SDK executes them → results feed back. Turns repeat automatically without returning control to your code. A simple query ('list files') may take 1–2 turns; a complex task ('refactor auth + update tests') can chain dozens. The SDK provides three guardrails: permission checks (allow/deny tool calls before they run), hooks (intercept or modify calls mid-flight), and cost/token limits that halt the loop if exceeded.
body_en.analogy: Think of the agent loop as a self-driving car on a highway. Each turn is one lane change or maneuver — the car handles them autonomously. Permissions are the guardrails on the road, hooks are your ability to grab the wheel mid-maneuver, and cost limits are the fuel gauge that forces the car to pull over.
